excerpt: Firmware-running RTL results currently include fifteen PicoRV32 wrapper smokes: failing and fixed images for sensor-threshold, interrupt-race, MMIO-ordering, stack-corruption, UART-command, and watchdog-timeout, plus selected no-failure sensor, UART-command, and watchdog edge cases. A generated smoke summary parses those wrapper logs and checks observed property IDs, capsule counts, freeze state, and overflow state. A seeded RTL-smoke interrupt campaign reruns selected interrupt-race schedules in fresh Icarus processes, checks capsule digest reproducibility, sweeps MMIO wait-state latency for interrupt-race cases, and verifies per-seed replay-comparator rejection of missing-event, duplicate-event, metadata, payload, and order corruptions. A generated alignment table checks that failing RTL-smoke capsules agree with the RV32I firmware interpreter on property IDs and key replay-visible event values. Full benchmark-wide RTL replay/export/compare coverage remains pending a broader PicoRV32 simulation harness, `make`/C++ support for Verilator-style builds, and a RISC-V compiler or equivalent firmware image flow.
guidance: Full RTL scope must be described as pending unless benchmark-wide RTL evidence exists.

excerpt: # ReplayCapsule-RV Novelty Matrix
guidance: Novelty language must be narrow, caveated, and tied to related-work evidence.

excerpt: This file states conservative, source-aware novelty language for ReplayCapsule-RV. It is aligned with the local architecture, event model, capsule format, research plan, and replay sufficiency theorem docs. It should be updated after RTL, firmware benchmarks, replay harness results, and synthesis/evaluation results are available. Until then, treat this as a claim-safety document rather than a final paper contribution section.
guidance: Novelty language must be narrow, caveated, and tied to related-work evidence.

excerpt: ## Safe Top-Line Novelty Statement
guidance: Novelty language must be narrow, caveated, and tied to related-work evidence.

excerpt: ReplayCapsule-RV is best positioned as a narrow embedded RISC-V replay artifact: a hardware/architecture prototype that captures and replays firmware-visible nondeterministic events needed to reproduce RV32I failures involving asynchronous interrupts and MMIO. The defensible novelty is the combination of an event-sufficiency replay contract, single-hart RV32I embedded scope, commit-indexed boundary events, and an interrupt/MMIO replay boundary. It should be framed as complementary to RISC-V trace/debug standards, assertion-based hardware verification, FPGA checkpoint/replay systems, and memory-safety hardware.
guidance: Novelty language must be narrow, caveated, and tied to related-work evidence.

It is scoped to deterministic replay capsules for RV32I embedded failures, especially replaying firmware-visible nondeterminism from interrupts and MMIO. | "First capsule architecture"; "stronger than Security Capsules"; "security validation"; "security proof"; any detailed statement about Security Capsules internals without the paper. | "Security Capsules show that capsule-like hardware assertion infrastructure is established for post-silicon security validation; ReplayCapsule-RV uses the capsule framing for a different contract: failure replay of RV32I interrupt/MMIO executions." |

excerpt: | Hassert: Hardware Assertion-Based Verification Framework with FPGA Acceleration | Verified high level. excerpt: | RISC-V E-Trace | Verified. Official RISC-V Efficient Trace for RISC-V v2.0 is ratified. It defines branch/delta trace, reporting PC discontinuities, branch outcomes, trap transitions, sync, optional timestamp/context/data payloads, and requires the decoder to have the program binary. | Efficient instruction trace for RISC-V: reconstructs instruction flow from a known start address plus encoded discontinuities. It covers jumps, branches, interrupts/exceptions that take traps, synchronization, filtering, timestamping, payload, and data-trace payload options. | Very close because it already traces interrupts/exceptions and can reconstruct the retired instruction stream. | E-Trace is a trace protocol, not by itself a deterministic replay contract. ReplayCapsule-RV's local contract does not store full instruction trace; it stores commit-indexed replay events such as `IRQ_LEVEL`, `MMIO_READ`, and `MMIO_WRITE`. E-Trace does not by itself guarantee MMIO read values, peripheral state, interrupt injection schedule relative to replay execution, or a compact failure capsule sufficient to drive an RV32I replay engine. | "E-Trace cannot handle interrupts"; "ReplayCapsule-RV replaces E-Trace"; "more compressed than E-Trace"; "supports all E-Trace semantics" without implementation. | "ReplayCapsule-RV is complementary to E-Trace: E-Trace reconstructs control flow; ReplayCapsule-RV focuses on replay-driving events, especially MMIO observations and interrupt delivery." |
guidance: Avoid first-ever/replacement claims unless the related-work evidence is complete.

excerpt: | ReTrace: VM deterministic replay for trace collection | Verified. excerpt: Embedded safety failures often depend on interrupt timing and memory-mapped I/O values that are difficult to reconstruct after the fact. ReplayCapsule-RV studies the minimum event boundary needed to reproduce such failures on a single-core RV32I embedded system. The prototype records nondeterministic MMIO inputs, interrupt timing, and property-relevant context into a compact hardware failure capsule that also drives runtime safety checking. This draft currently includes the formal model, RTL capture scaffold, generated proof-obligation matrix, bounded event-tap, event-classifier/slicer, property-checker, hash-signature, MMIO/interrupt logger, register, replay-control, replay-mismatch, recorder, and capsule-buffer proof/cover checks, fifteen PicoRV32 wrapper firmware smokes, a seeded RTL-smoke interrupt reproducibility campaign, twelve failing/fixed RTL-smoke capsule export self-compare, missing-event, duplicate-event, metadata-corruption, payload-corruption, order-corruption, PC-context, and event-class ablation checks, bounded overflow contract checks, RTL/firmware-sim alignment rows, generic Yosys cell counts with derived generic overhead context, and evaluation scripts; full benchmark RTL replay and mapped FPGA timing/resource numbers are pending real tool runs.
guidance: Mapped hardware and timing metrics must remain TODO/NA until backed by real tool reports.

excerpt: Embedded firmware failures often depend on the order in which interrupts and memory-mapped I/O values reach a single-core processor. Full traces can capture that behavior, but they are costly to store and awkward to replay. ReplayCapsule-RV explores whether a single-hart RV32I target can replay safety failures from an event-sufficient capsule that records commit-indexed firmware-visible nondeterminism introduced by interrupts and MMIO. The artifact includes a model, an RV32I firmware interpreter, record-side RTL, PicoRV32 wrapper smokes, host-driven RTL replay rows, negative comparator tests, bounded local checks, scoped tiny iCE40 mapped rows, and generated baselines for six bug families. Compiler-backed firmware, full-core mapped overhead, true runtime overhead, and synthesizable replay-consume claims are not made unless generated by the corresponding scripts.
guidance: Mapped hardware and timing metrics must remain TODO/NA until backed by real tool reports.

excerpt: An event contains a commit index, a same-index order tag, a class, and a payload. Core classes are those required to drive replay in the scoped failures: MMIO read values, MMIO write observations, interrupt entry and exit, external inputs, selected stores or branch outcomes when they influence the property, checkpoint/hash values when used, and the property-failure signature. Diagnostic PC context is useful for debugging and comparator alignment, but the model does not claim global trace minimality.
guidance: Minimality claims must be replaced by sufficient/capsule-scope language unless proved.

excerpt: Processor trace standards and debug fabrics, including RISC-V trace work and vendor debug systems, aim to reconstruct program execution with rich control-flow or instruction-level visibility~\cite{riscvTrace,riscvDebug}. ReplayCapsule-RV does not replace those mechanisms. It asks whether a scoped embedded failure can be replayed from the smaller set of firmware-visible boundary facts that drive the property failure.

excerpt: ReplayCapsule-RV defines and implements an event-sufficiency model for deterministic replay of embedded RV32I failures caused by asynchronous interrupts and memory-mapped I/O. The same lightweight hardware event stream drives runtime safety checking and compact replay-capsule generation without relying on full instruction traces or full-system snapshots.
